scoring_notes.spam_reason_specific_and_evidence_based: The reason field cites four distinct, concrete signals: (1) service offering with no project intent, (2) unknown sender identity, (3) a specific suspicious URL (seguidores-fake.com), and (4) bulk/automated messaging pattern. This is maximally specific and grounded in observable evidence from the message.
scoring_notes.all_spam_signals_identified: All recognizable spam signals present in the inbound message are captured: commercial offer, absence of qualifying project fields (location, client name, design intent), a promotional external link, and automated/bulk message characteristics. No signals were overlooked.
scoring_notes.classification_decision_unambiguous: The classification field is 'spam' with no hedging or conditional language. The reason statement directly maps each signal to a spam criterion, leaving no ambiguity about the decision or its basis.
